element_introduction_order.mastery_context: fast, low damage, harder to block but uses less stamina per execution
slow, high damage/knockback, easier to defend against, more stamina cost
must read opponent positioning and strike undefended areas
when opponent reaches 0 stamina, all attacks deal strong punch knockback; resource management becomes critical
random hazard dealing 5 stamina damage and temporary stun; introduces unpredictability
resets both fighters to starting positions at random; interrupts momentum
police arrest fighter nearest edge if time expires; incentivizes aggression
pushes opponent into manhole = round/match win; appears only in endgame scenarios

typical_relief_triggers: patrol car arrival (resets fighters, breaks engagement)
typical_tension_triggers: opponent pushing player toward screen edge
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: exchange-based tension: builds through trading hits and position shifts; periodic relief from environmental interrupts (patrol car) or round completion; overall steady rather than pulsing

difficulty_curve.early_game: single-player Game A begins with opponent matched to player skill; early matches introduce core mechanics without overwhelming complexity
difficulty_curve.mid_game: after 2 rounds lost, sewer manhole hazard appears; match stakes escalate as player progresses toward 45 consecutive victories for Champion rank
difficulty_curve.late_game: sustained pressure maintaining winning streak; difficulty increases through accumulated opponent variety and stamina management demands
notable_spikes: introduction of sewer manhole (round 3+ of each match)
cumulative fatigue from consecutive victory requirement (45 wins needed for Champion)

notable_level_patterns.description: each round shifts to a new block/street location after opponent knocked off sidewalk; creates spatial variety within same core mechanics
notable_level_patterns.example_level: game progresses through multiple blocks as player accumulates victories

notable_level_patterns.description: best-of-five format per match ensures multiple tension cycles per opponent; sewer manhole only appears in final rounds when stakes are highest
flower pots and patrol cars introduce external disruption without changing core one-on-one combat rules; hazards are random rather than scripted
notable_level_patterns.example_level: persistent across all rounds and matches
